todaysEvents.summary: US-Iran fire exchange May 7: IRGC fired missiles, drones, and fast boats at USS Truxtun, USS Rafael Peralta, and USS Mason east of Hormuz near Chabahar; US responded with self-defense strikes, sinking 7 small Iranian boats; each side claims the other fired first; Project Freedom was paused May 5 but destroyers remained in strait (CBS News, CNBC, PBS, Axios, May 7).
todaysEvents.delta: Up: First direct US-Iran naval engagement since crisis began; each side blames the other; fragile ceasefire survived but miscalculation risk is now live with both militaries in contact

todaysEvents.summary: Trump: ceasefire holds; fire exchange was 'just a love tap'; Iran reviewing 14-point MOU (12-year nuclear moratorium, competing blockades lifted, 30-day detailed talks window); Iranian FM Araghchi expected to convey response via Pakistan within 48 hours; Trump: 'very possible we'll make a deal' (CNBC, NBC, Axios, May 7-8).
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Both sides stepping back from escalation framing; MOU framework remains active; Pakistan facilitating response; Iran has not declared ceasefire terminated

todaysEvents.summary: Brent +1.2% to $101.26 May 8 (CNBC; rallied on fire exchange fear, off D65 low $101.27); WTI +0.9% to $95.64; TTF €44.84 (D66 confirmed, carried); JKM $16.84 (D66 confirmed, carried); tail 35% (eased from 45% on MOU progress; fire exchange prevents further compression) (May 8).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Oil rally modest; market treating fire exchange as contained incident; 14-point MOU is the dominant price signal for the week; Hormuz ~90% closed; mine-clearing 6 months on any deal

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: 14-point MOU (12-year nuclear moratorium, competing blockades lifted in parallel, 30-day detailed talks) reaches consensus via Pakistan; fire exchange on May 7 is treated as isolated incident; Iran conveys positive response within 48hrs; Hormuz mine-clearing begins. Brent retreats toward $88-95/bbl on confirmed MOU signature; iTraxx tightens toward 165bp. Narrow (15%) - Iran 'strongly rejected' some MOU terms; Rezaei demands reparations; Trump threatens to restart strikes; fire exchange demonstrates enforcement risk even on ceasefire.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: MOU framework remains active but Iran's response via Pakistan reveals sticking points on nuclear moratorium length or reparations; 30-day window opens without guaranteed outcome; fire exchange on May 7 contained under Trump 'love tap' framing; partial Hormuz access continues under ceasefire with both blockades nominally in place. Brent stabilises $95-110/bbl; TTF €42-47/MWh; JKM $15-19/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 175-210bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (50%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Fire exchange on May 7 is the opening of a wider engagement - Iran determines US 'love tap' response justifies ceasefire termination; IRGC resumes full Hormuz interdiction and attacks US destroyers; nuclear MOU collapses; Gulf-wide blockade tightens. Brent spikes $125-170/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting brings Saudi/UAE output under acute threat; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (35%) - lower than D59 peak 55% reflecting MOU progress, but fire exchange demonstrates that miscalculation risk inside Hormuz remains live.
